description_long: This is a small, vintage-style biplane with a wooden appearance and a two-tone color scheme of tan and reddish-brown. The aircraft features two stacked wings supported by struts, a single front-mounted propeller, and a fixed tailwheel landing gear. The fuselage is compact, with an open cockpit situated just behind the upper wing, and the tail section includes a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers. The propeller and engine cowling are visible at the nose, and the landing gear consists of two main wheels and a small tailwheel. The wings have a slight dihedral angle, and the overall design is reminiscent of early 20th-century sport or trainer aircraft. The surface finish appears matte, with visible panel lines and structural supports.
description: A small, vintage-style biplane with tan and reddish-brown coloring, featuring stacked wings, an open cockpit, and a front-mounted propeller.
description_view_0: Side-rear view showing the tail section, single vertical stabilizer, and the open cockpit behind the upper wing.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the dual stacked wings, struts, and the full wingspan with visible color transitions.
description_view_2: Opposite side-rear view, again showing the tail, landing gear, and cockpit placement, with the propeller partially visible.
description_view_3: Frontal view displaying the propeller, engine cowling, and the symmetrical biplane wing structure.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two straight, parallel wings stacked vertically and supported by struts; moderate dihedral angle; no winglets.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with a two-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Taildragger configuration with two main fixed wheels and a single tailwheel.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Open, single-seat cockpit located just behind the upper wing, forward-mounted on the fuselage.
